name: U.S. Presidents
description: Commanders-in-chief, their lives, and presidential history

questions.q: Which U.S. President was known by the nickname 'Old Hickory'?

source.notes: Nickname came from his toughness as a military commander. 7th President.

questions.q: Before becoming President, Ronald Reagan served as governor of which state?

source.notes: Governor 1967-1975. Before that he was a Hollywood actor.

questions.q: Which U.S. President was known by the nickname 'Ike'?

source.notes: 34th President, 1953-1961. Supreme Allied Commander in WWII.

questions.q: What was the name of President Richard Nixon's famous family dog, referenced in a 1952 speech?

source.notes: The 'Checkers speech' saved his spot on the 1952 ticket as Eisenhower's running mate.

questions.q: What was President Jimmy Carter's middle name?

source.notes: James Earl Carter Jr. Born 1924 in Plains, Georgia.

questions.q: In which U.S. state was President Jimmy Carter born?

source.notes: Plains, Georgia. Famously ran a peanut farming business.

questions.q: At 6 feet 4 inches, who was the tallest U.S. President?

source.notes: 16th President. LBJ was second tallest at 6'3.5".

questions.q: At 5 feet 4 inches, who was the shortest U.S. President?

source.notes: 4th President. Often called the 'Father of the Constitution.'

questions.q: Calvin Coolidge was famously sworn in as President by whom in 1923?

source.notes: Father John Coolidge was a notary public. Ceremony held in Plymouth, Vermont after Harding's death.

questions.q: Aside from John F. Kennedy, which other U.S. President is buried at Arlington National Cemetery?

source.notes: 27th President and later Chief Justice. Died 1930.

questions.q: How many times was Franklin D. Roosevelt elected to the presidency?

source.notes: 1932, 1936, 1940, 1944. Led to 22nd Amendment (1951) limiting presidents to 2 terms.

questions.q: In which city was Senator Robert F. Kennedy assassinated in 1968?

source.notes: Shot at the Ambassador Hotel on June 5, 1968. Died the next day.

questions.q: Which U.S. state was George W. Bush visiting a school in when he was told about the 9/11 attacks?

source.notes: Emma E. Booker Elementary School, Sarasota. Reading 'The Pet Goat' to a 2nd grade class.

questions.q: In what U.S. city was President William McKinley assassinated in 1901?

source.notes: Shot at the Pan-American Exposition by anarchist Leon Czolgosz. Died 8 days later.

questions.q: Who was the first President of the United States?

source.notes: Inaugurated April 30, 1789 in New York City. Served two terms.

questions.q: In what Texas city was President John F. Kennedy assassinated on November 22, 1963?

source.notes: Shot in Dealey Plaza while riding in a motorcade.

questions.q: Which four U.S. Presidents are carved into Mount Rushmore?

source.notes: Carved 1927-1941 by Gutzon Borglum in the Black Hills of South Dakota.

questions.q: Who was the first U.S. President to resign from office?

source.notes: Resigned August 9, 1974 due to the Watergate scandal.

questions.q: Who was the first African American President of the United States?

source.notes: 44th President, served 2009-2017. Born in Honolulu, Hawaii.

questions.q: Grover Cleveland is the only U.S. President to have served two terms in what unusual way?

source.notes: 22nd and 24th President (1885-89, 1893-97). Also the 47th (Donald Trump, 2025) matches this feat.

questions.q: Who was the youngest person ever elected President of the United States?

source.notes: 43 years old when elected (1960). TR was 42 when he became President but had assumed office after McKinley's assassination.

questions.q: Kamala Harris made history in 2021 by becoming what first?

source.notes: Sworn in January 20, 2021 as VP under Joe Biden. Previously a U.S. Senator from California.
